title: Probing Quantum Entanglement in $τ^+τ^-$ Pairs via the $ππ$ Channel at STCF
authors: Xiaokang Li, Chentao Bao, Hai Chen, Mingyi Liu, Dayong Wang
abstract: Quantum entanglement and Bell-inequality violation in $τ^+τ^-$ pairs provide a sensitive probe of quantum correlations in high-energy interactions. We present a feasibility study of $e^+e^- \to τ^+τ^-$ at the proposed Super Tau-Charm Facility based on full Monte Carlo simulation at $\sqrt{s} = 7$ GeV, focusing on the $ππ$ channel ($τ^\pm \to π^\pmν$), which offers the maximal spin-analyzing power $|κ| = 1$ and the simplest final-state topology for validating the quantum-tomography framework. We establish a consistency chain from the tree-level QED prediction through truth-level and detector-level reconstruction, yielding a reconstructed concurrence of $0.279 \pm 0.007$ with the good-solution approach. A complementary full-simulation study of the $ρρ$ channel is also briefly reported. These results demonstrate that the STCF can provide a competitive platform for precision studies of quantum correlations in $τ$-lepton pairs.
summary: This paper presents a feasibility study for detecting quantum entanglement in tau-lepton pairs at the future Super Tau-Charm Facility. By simulating the pi-pi decay channel, the authors demonstrate that the facility can accurately reconstruct quantum correlations and validate the quantum-tomography framework. This establishes STCF as a competitive platform for high-energy particle physics studies involving quantum information observables.
main_problem: Assessing the feasibility of using the proposed Super Tau-Charm Facility (STCF) to probe quantum entanglement and Bell-inequality violation in tau-lepton pairs.
main_result: The study successfully established a consistency chain for reconstructing entanglement, achieving a reconstructed concurrence of 0.279 ± 0.007 in the pi-pi channel, demonstrating STCF's potential for precision quantum correlation studies.
method: Full Monte Carlo simulations using the OSCAR framework, including Geant4-based detector simulation, and quantum state tomography via the polarimeter-vector method.
model_or_system: The physical system consists of tau-lepton pairs (tau+tau-) produced via electron-positron annihilation at a center-of-mass energy of 7 GeV at the proposed Super Tau-Charm Facility (STCF).
key_observables: Concurrence (C), Bell-inequality violation parameter (m12), Spin Density Matrix (SDM) elements, and spin-analyzing power (kappa).
important_parameters: Center-of-mass energy sqrt(s) = 7 GeV; peak luminosity of 0.5 x 10^35 cm^-2s^-1; tau decay length of approximately 140 micrometers.
main_assumptions: The study uses a 'good-solution' approach to handle the two-fold ambiguity in tau momentum reconstruction, effectively isolating detector effects from reconstruction errors.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows SM predictions for Concurrence and the Horodecki condition as functions of energy and scattering angle; Figure 2 displays angular distributions of decay products along specific axes.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific motivation, describes the experimental platform (STCF) and simulation framework, details the reconstruction methodology and the consistency chain, presents results for the pi-pi and rho-rho channels, and discusses limitations regarding vertex resolution.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1: SM predictions for C (left), m12 (middle), and C and m12 at √s = 7 GeV (right).

Fig. 2: Distributions of cos θ± i = −h± i (top two rows) and cos θ− i cos θ+ j = h− i h+ j (bottom one row) along the diagonal {ˆn, ˆr, ˆk} directions in the ππ channel, com- pared among the full-PHSP truth sample (red histogram), the in-acceptance truth sample (blue histogram), and the corresponding good-solution reconstruction (green histogram).
